title: Portland Pivot Kick Study on Medial Meniscus Tears and Partial Meniscectomy
author: Samuel Moulton
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7d2hi8e
materials: The study timeline is as follows:

steps.title: Initial visit
steps.instruction: Initial visit : The patient will be evaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. Arthroscopic partial meniscectomy will be offered as a surgical option to the patient. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.

steps.title: 6 week post operative follow up
steps.instruction: 6 week post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.

steps.title: 6 month post operative follow up
steps.instruction: 6 month post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.

steps.title: 1 year post operative follow up
steps.instruction: 1 year post operative follow up: The patient will be reevaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.

steps.title: The patient will perform the following...
steps.instruction: The patient will perform the following home exercises during the postoperative period:

steps.title: Preoperative visit
steps.instruction: Preoperative visit: The patient will be evaluated by physical examination including the PPK maneuver. Arthroscopic partial meniscectomy will be offered as a surgical option to the patient. The patient will fill out the WOMAC and KOOS questionnaire.
